I dont think it should have to be necessarily dark themed, but it needs to still have the mysterious and strength personified aspect to them. Much like Lugia and HoOh (especially since the sea and the sky are legitimately related, if not attached/tied, to waves and wind). Pokemon 2000 did an incredible job of making Lugia appear just slightly scary because of the almost folklore historical legends they told about it (along with sea serpent quick teaser moments hinting at its appearance with that hauntingly beautiful song). The final reveal made viewers awestruck because it showed that Lugia was truly a benevolent yet numinous Pokemon. It still was creating massive weather storms, changing currents throughout the entire ocean, and midair whirlpools while somehow equally holding its own battling 3 other legendary Pokemon!Keeping that allure of how exotic/rare, powerful/potentially dangerous, and realization that this isnt a Pokemon thats meant to be caught because its a force of nature makes legendary Pokemon truly standout. I think Pokemon has lost that original and important impactful symbolism ever since diamond/pearl ended.While I believe some legendary Pokemon shouldnt be cute and have their own place in that world with darker undertones and natural evil morality, I agree these ones dont have to be dark Pokemon. But I sure hope they return to their roots of what made Lugia and HoOh so unique, mysterious, and dominating forces in the Pokemon universe!
